title: Migrate archive state from localStorage to SQLite
description: Add an archivedAt timestamp column (nullable) to the characters table via a migration script. Update the archive handler to write to this column via a PATCH or dedicated POST endpoint. Update the restore handler to null the column. Update the Active Character dropdown query to filter WHERE archivedAt IS NULL. Update the Archived panel query to filter WHERE archivedAt IS NOT NULL. Remove localStorage reads/writes for archive state entirely. The behavior from the user's perspective must be identical — only the persistence layer changes.
close_reason: Added archived_at column via MIGRATIONS, archiveCharacter/restoreCharacter in repositories, /api/character-archive + /api/character-restore routes, removed localStorage archivedIds state from CastingPipelinePanel.

title: Fix Journey A promotion path to Active Character
description: Based on audit findings: if Journey A characters cannot reach Active Character, implement a promotion mechanism. 'Select this look' should save the selected candidate as a character in the database (same schema as Journey B cast characters) and make them available in the Active Character dropdown. If the code path exists but is broken, fix the specific breakage. Minimum: after clicking 'Select this look', the character must appear in Active Character and be usable for Portfolio generation. Do not change the Journey B flow.
close_reason: Journey A characters already in characters table at generation time. Added scroll-to-active-char + confirmation message after 'Select this look'.

title: Audit archive implementation
description: Find every place 'archive' or 'archived' is written or read in the codebase. Confirm whether archive state is in localStorage, SQLite, or both. Find the archive button handler, the restore handler, and the Active Character dropdown filter. Output: a written finding with exact file and line references.
close_reason: Archive 100% localStorage. No archivedAt column in characters table. Full migration needed.

title: Audit Journey A promotion path
description: Trace what happens after 'Select this look' is clicked. Find the handler, trace what state it sets, and determine whether Journey A characters ever reach the Active Character dropdown. Output: a written finding — either (a) Journey A characters DO reach Active Character via a code path not documented in the guide, or (b) they genuinely cannot and the flow is broken. Do not fix anything in this task. Find and report only.
close_reason: Journey A characters ARE created in characters table at runAudition() time. Flow works — gap is UI feedback only.

title: Casting Room flow fixes
description: Fix all critical and significant flow issues identified in flow analysis v2. Two journeys must both have complete, consistent paths to Portfolio and Gallery. Archive must be persisted properly. Action labels must have clear, documented outcomes.
close_reason: Epic open — work in progress

title: Generate Previews should target only approved candidates, not pending
description:
Why: handleGeneratePreviews() filters candidates by reviewStatus !== 'rejected', which includes pending candidates. Calling saveBatchCandidate on a pending candidate immediately changes its reviewStatus to 'saved' and refreshBatch re-renders the card with 'Saved ✓' and no Cast/Dismiss buttons — the user loses the ability to review those candidates before committing them.

What needs to be done:
- Change the targets filter in handleGeneratePreviews from:
    candidates.filter((c) => c.reviewStatus !== 'rejected' && !batchPreviewJobs[c.id])
  to:
    candidates.filter((c) => c.reviewStatus === 'approved' && !batchPreviewJobs[c.id])
- Update the button disabled condition to match: disabled when no approved candidates remain unqueued.
- If no candidates are approved yet, show a hint near the button: 'Cast characters first, then generate previews.'

File: src/components/CastingPipelinePanel.jsx — handleGeneratePreviews() and the Generate Previews button disabled prop.
close_reason: Filter changed to reviewStatus === 'approved' in both handler and disabled condition. Hint text added when no candidates are approved yet.

title: Persistent render status bar showing live polling progress
description:
Why: Polling runs completely invisibly. Users have no idea how many renders are pending, running, or done.

What needs to be done:
- Create a new RenderStatusBar component (src/components/CastingRoom/RenderStatusBar.jsx or inline in CastingPipelinePanel.jsx).
- Show it at the top of the Casting Room panel whenever isPollingAudit or isPollingPortfolio is true.
- Content: 'Rendering images • N of M complete • Audition: X/Y • Portfolio: A/B'
- Compute counts from: auditionStatuses (count 'success' vs total), portfolioJobsStatus.items (count 'success' vs total), moreTakesState (flatten all jobStatuses across all characterIds).
- Show a spinner/pulse animation while active, green checkmark when all done.
- Add a separate subsection collapse: 'Active Audition Renders (3 pending)' and 'Active Portfolio Renders (2 pending)' expandable below the bar.

Key files: src/components/CastingPipelinePanel.jsx. State variables: isPollingAudit (line ~22), isPollingPortfolio (line ~48), auditionStatuses (line ~21), portfolioJobsStatus (line ~49), moreTakesState (line ~24). CSS: src/components/CastingPipelinePanel.module.css.
close_reason: Phase 1 complete: CSS + JS implemented, clean build

title: Combined 'Approve + Generate Portfolio' single-click action
description:
Why: The most common next step after approving an audition is generating a full portfolio. This should be one click, not four steps.

What needs to be done:
- Add a new handler handleApproveAndGeneratePortfolio(auditionId, characterId) in CastingPipelinePanel.jsx that: (1) calls approveActorAudition(auditionId), (2) calls setSelectedCharacterId(characterId), (3) compiles prompt packs for the character (compilePromptPacksForCharacter), (4) calls queueCharacterPortfolio with the current portfolioViews and selectedWorkflowId, (5) starts portfolio polling.
- Add an 'Approve + Portfolio' button to each audition result card in the render section (alongside the existing Approve and Reject buttons).
- This button should be disabled if selectedWorkflowId is not set, with a tooltip explaining why.
- After the action, scroll to the gallery section so the user can watch images appear.

Key files: src/components/CastingPipelinePanel.jsx. handleQueuePortfolio (line 513) has the portfolio queuing logic to extract. handleApproveAudition (line 413) has the approve logic.
close_reason: Phase 1 complete: CSS + JS implemented, clean build

title: Auto-select character + 'Generate Portfolio?' toast after approve/save
description:
Why: After approving an audition or saving a batch candidate, nothing guides the user toward the next step (generating a portfolio). They have to manually scroll and find the character.

What needs to be done:
- In handleApproveAudition(auditionId): after calling approveActorAudition, find the characterId for that audition from auditionResults (auditionResults is an array; each result has characterId and a views array with auditionId). Auto-call setSelectedCharacterId(characterId) and scroll to activeCharSectionRef.
- Add a postApprovalPrompt state: { characterId, characterName } — set it after approve or save actions.
- Render a toast/banner near the Active Character section when postApprovalPrompt is set: 'Character approved. Ready to generate portfolio?' with a one-click 'Generate Full Portfolio' button (calls handleQueuePortfolio) and a dismiss button.
- Apply the same auto-select + toast after handleCandidateAction('save') — this already scrolls but does not set the character or show the prompt.

Key files: src/components/CastingPipelinePanel.jsx. handleApproveAudition is at line 413, handleCandidateAction at line 446, handleQueuePortfolio at line 513. The auditionResults state maps results to characterIds.
close_reason: Phase 1 complete: CSS + JS implemented, clean build

title: Auto-compile prompt packs on character creation
description:
Why: Users must manually click 'Compile prompt packs' after a character is created, breaking the flow. This should happen silently in the background.

What needs to be done:
- In CastingPipelinePanel.jsx handleGenerateAudition(): after the auditionResults arrive, fire background compile calls (compilePromptPacksForCharacter) for each successfully created characterId without blocking the UI. Track compiling state per characterId in a new compilingPrompts state (a Set or map).
- In handleCandidateAction('save'): after saving a batch candidate, auto-compile prompt packs for the returned characterId.
- Add compile status display in the Active Character section: 'Compiling prompts...' → 'Prompts ready' instead of the current silent state.
- Remove or demote the manual 'Compile prompt packs' button to a 'Recompile' option only shown when packs already exist.

Key files: src/components/CastingPipelinePanel.jsx (lines 413-420 handleApproveAudition, lines 446-480 handleCandidateAction, lines 486-497 handleCompileAndListPromptPacks). API call is compilePromptPacksForCharacter from src/lib/api/promptPacks.js.
close_reason: Phase 1 complete: CSS + JS implemented, clean build

title: EPIC: Casting Room workflow overhaul
description: Transform the Casting Room from two disconnected paths into one fluid, visual, automated casting pipeline. See Casting_room_Logic_Improvments.md for full spec and Casting_room_Logic.md for codebase context. Three phases: Phase 1 (high-impact automation), Phase 2 (lifecycle + unified UI), Phase 3 (infrastructure).
notes: Phase 1 issues (start here): jhq (auto-compile), 35y (toast+auto-select), 7om (approve+portfolio), 853 (status bar), 57f (quick previews). Phase 2: hv3 (lifecycle states), c6z (CharacterCard), ai6 (more takes on rejected). Phase 3: hv7 (websocket), 4ld (render priorities). Start with jhq — it is the foundation that 35y and 7om both depend on.
close_reason: All three phases complete: Phase 1 (auto-compile, auto-select, approve+portfolio, status bar, quick previews), Phase 2 (lifecycle status, CharacterCard, more-takes on rejected), Phase 3 (SSE real-time updates, render priority tiers). All sub-issues closed.

title: Scroll to Active Character after batch Save
description:
Why it's broken: When a Journey A audition is approved (handleApproveAudition), the panel scrolls to the Active Character section via activeCharSectionRef.current?.scrollIntoView(). Journey B's Save → Active Character (handleCandidateAction 'save' branch, ~line 457) sets selectedCharacterId and shows a success banner but does NOT scroll. The user has to manually scroll down to see the Active Character section updated.

Fix: In handleCandidateAction, after the 'save' action success path sets setSuccess(), add:
setTimeout(() => activeCharSectionRef.current?.scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth', block: 'nearest' }), 100)

File: src/components/CastingPipelinePanel.jsx — handleCandidateAction (~line 468).
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix candidate button states for all reviewStatus values
description:
Why it's broken: The candidate action buttons are gated only on reviewStatus !== 'rejected', so candidates with reviewStatus='saved' still show Cast this character + Dismiss buttons — which is confusing since the character is already in Active Character.

Expected per-status behavior:
- pending: Cast this character + Dismiss
- approved: hide Cast (already done), show Save → Active Character (prominent) + Dismiss
- saved: show only 'saved ✓' badge, no action buttons
- rejected: show only Reconsider (already correct)

File: src/components/CastingPipelinePanel.jsx — the candidate rendering block (~lines 837-851). Replace the single reviewStatus !== 'rejected' guard with explicit per-status branches.
close_reason: Closed

title: Auto-select new batch after generation
description:
Why it's broken: handleGenerateBatch() (CastingPipelinePanel.jsx ~line 572) refreshes the batch list and sets batches state but never sets selectedBatchId. The dropdown stays on 'Select a batch…', the success banner says 'select it below', and the user has to hunt for the new batch manually.

Fix: listCharacterBatches() returns rows ORDER BY created_at DESC so batchResult.items[0] is the newest batch. After setBatches(batchResult.items), add: if (batchResult.items?.[0]?.id) setSelectedBatchId(batchResult.items[0].id)

File: src/components/CastingPipelinePanel.jsx — handleGenerateBatch function (~line 588).
close_reason: Closed

title: Batch Pipeline — fix UX and core flow issues
description:
The Batch Pipeline section in the Casting Room tab has several bugs and UX gaps that make it feel broken. Root causes identified via code audit and API testing:

1. After generating a batch the dropdown stays on 'Select a batch…' — user has to find and select it manually.
2. Batch dropdown shows truncated UUIDs instead of date/count/tone — impossible to identify batches.
3. After 'Cast this character' (approve), no visual cue guides user to click 'Save → Active Character'. The Cast button is still visible even though it's redundant.
4. After 'Save → Active Character' succeeds, the Cast + Dismiss buttons are still rendered (reviewStatus='saved' is not filtered out of the action button block).
5. No scroll-to-Active-Character after batch save — Journey A scrolls down, Journey B does not.
6. Global actionLoading disables all candidate cards while any one action is running.
7. Success message says 'Candidate reconsiderd.' (typo).
8. Selected batch summary line shows only raw ID + status, not useful counts.
close_reason: All 8 items addressed: (1) auto-select newest batch post-generate, (2) dropdown shows date/count/tone, (3) approval hint + accent button added, (4) saved candidates hide action buttons, (5) scroll-to-active-char on batch save, (6) per-candidate actionLoading replaced, (7) reconsider typo was already fixed in Phase 1 with explicit message, (8) batch summary shows candidate counts.

title: Build ActorDetail component — character detail panel
description:
Why: Clicking a character card in ActorBankView only updates selected state but renders nothing. Phase 7 of the Actor Bank brief was never implemented. The tab is non-interactive.

What to build: src/components/ActorBank/ActorDetail.jsx + ActorDetail.module.css

Behaviour: When a character is selected in ActorBankView, replace the grid with the detail view (Option B from the brief — simpler, no z-index). Back button returns to grid and preserves filter state.

Detail panel layout (from brief):
- Back arrow + character name (large)
- Age · Gender · Archetype
- REFERENCE IMAGES section: all generated images for this character in a horizontal scroll. Each image uses /api/generated-image-view?id=<imageId> as src. Show view label below each.
- CHARACTER PROFILE section: render the character payload fields as readable key-value pairs (not raw JSON). Key fields: ethnicityOrRegionalLook, faceShape, eyes, hairColor, bodyType, posture, distinctiveFeatures, wardrobeBase, cinematicArchetype, personalityEnergy, visualKeywords.
- No editing or archive in v1 — read-only display is sufficient to make the tab useful.

Props: { character: object, images: [], onBack: fn }

Integration in ActorBankView: when selected !== null, fetch /api/characters?id=selected (the endpoint from the previous task), then render <ActorDetail> instead of the grid. Show loading skeleton while fetching. On error, show inline error with back button.

Files: ActorDetail.jsx, ActorDetail.module.css, ActorBankView.jsx (add fetch on select + conditional render)
close_reason: Created ActorDetail.jsx + ActorDetail.module.css. Updated ActorBankView.jsx: added handleSelect (fetches /api/characters?id=, sets detail state), handleBack (clears detail), three render branches (loading skeleton, error+back, detail panel). ActorDetail shows back button, name/age/gender/archetype header, horizontal image strip using imageUrl from API, and profile grid grouped into Face/Skin&Hair/Body/Screen presence/Distinctive features/Visual keywords sections.

title: Add GET /api/characters?id=<id> single-character endpoint with images
description:
Why: The ActorDetail panel (next task) needs to load a single character with all their generated images. Currently /api/characters only supports a list query. Need to handle the case where the 'id' query param is present.

What to change: In vite.config.js, the GET /api/characters handler — before the list logic, check if url.searchParams.get('id') is non-empty. If so, call getCharacter(runtime.db, id) (getCharacter is already imported via repositories.js), then listGeneratedImageRecords(runtime.db, { characterId: id }), enrich each image with imageUrl: '/api/generated-image-view?id=' + img.id, return { ok: true, item: { ...characterPayload, images: [...] } }. Return 404 if character not found.

Files: vite.config.js only. getCharacter and listGeneratedImageRecords are both already imported.
close_reason: Added single-character branch to GET /api/characters handler (vite.config.js lines 255-268). When ?id= param is present, returns { ok, item: { ...character, images: [...] } } where each image includes imageUrl. Also added getCharacter to the repositories import. Verified: single-char returns ok+item+images with imageUrl, 404 on unknown id, list path unaffected.

title: Enrich /api/characters response with thumbnailUrl per character
description:
Why: ActorBankView fetches /api/characters and passes thumbnailUrl to ActorCard. Currently the endpoint returns only the character payload_json from SQLite which has no image data — all 8 cards show grey placeholders.

What to change: In vite.config.js, the GET /api/characters handler (line ~237) after calling listCharacters(), query listGeneratedImageRecords(runtime.db, { characterId: c.id }) for each character, pick the first front_portrait image (or any image if no front_portrait exists), then inject thumbnailUrl: '/api/generated-image-view?id=' + image.id into each character object. Characters with no images get thumbnailUrl: null (placeholder already handled in ActorCard).

Files: vite.config.js (handler only — no schema changes needed). listGeneratedImageRecords is already imported.
close_reason: Added thumbnail enrichment to GET /api/characters handler in vite.config.js (lines 262-274). Loads all generated_images in one query, groups by characterId, picks front_portrait first then any image, injects thumbnailUrl as /api/generated-image-view?id=... into each character. Verified: API returns thumbnailUrl for all 8 characters, image proxy responds 200 image/png.

(2) No detail panel implemented (Phase 7 from the brief was never built) — clicking a card only sets local selected state but renders nothing. Both issues make the tab appear completely broken. No code changes yet — this epic tracks the fix work.

description: Journey A auto-saves character profiles to the DB during Generate Auditions (via createCharacter in auditionOrchestrator.js). The Active Character dropdown populates from listCharacters() called in initialLoad(). Journey A characters should therefore auto-appear — but this has not been verified end-to-end. If they don't appear (e.g. because initialLoad() runs before audition completes, or characters are filtered out), Journey A has no path to the Portfolio step. Fix: verify the full flow, and if needed refresh the character list after handleGenerateAudition completes. Update the guide to confirm characters auto-appear.
close_reason: listCharacters() called after handleGenerateAudition; savedCharacters state refreshed. First new character auto-selected in Active Character dropdown. Lines 328-340.

title: Fix Journey A ghost images: eliminate double-render from audition auto-queue + Portfolio step
description: Journey A auto-queues two Comfy jobs (front_portrait, profile_portrait) during Generate Auditions. These images land in the DB and appear in the Gallery. The convergence section then has Queue Portfolio which queues the same views again. A Journey A user who runs both steps ends up with two sets of images in the Gallery for the same character with no visual distinction. Decision required: (a) Journey A users skip the Portfolio queue step — their audition images are already in the Gallery, or (b) Journey A does not auto-queue Comfy jobs — it only generates the character profile, and the user queues images via the Portfolio step. Option (a) is the smaller change. Implement whichever is chosen and update the guide.
close_reason: Option (a): Journey A keeps auto-queue. Added: auto-select first generated character; informational note explaining front+profile are queued and how to avoid duplicates in Portfolio. Lines 558-563.

title: Fix More Takes gate: unlock panel on terminal Comfy state, not user approval
description: In CastingPipelinePanel.jsx, the More Takes panel is conditionally rendered only when result.views.some(v => auditionItemActions[v.auditionId]?.status === 'approved'). This means if both auto-queued Comfy jobs fail (bad output, ComfyUI error), the user can never approve a view and the More Takes panel never appears — they have no in-app path to request additional renders for a failed candidate. The gate should be: show More Takes when all views for a pair have reached a terminal Comfy status (success OR failed), regardless of approval state. Approval and requesting retakes are orthogonal actions.
close_reason: Gate changed to: show MoreTakesPanel when all views have reached terminal Comfy state (success or failed), not on user approval. Views without comfyPromptId count as terminal immediately. Lines 607-613.

These must ship before any other casting room work.

Stop polling when all jobs are success or failed. Add per-view status badges (spinner / checkmark / error).
close_reason: Closed

title: Debug: LM Studio audition timeout — fix JSON mode, max_tokens, thinking suppression
description:
User hits 'Local LM Studio request timed out' when pressing Generate Audition. Root causes identified:

1. **Thinking mode not properly suppressed**: lmStudioProvider sends /no_think as text prefix, but LM Studio 0.3+ supports enable_thinking=false API param for Qwen3 models. If the GGUF lacks the /no_think token, the model does full CoT reasoning and burns through max_tokens before outputting JSON — causing 120s timeout.

2. **max_tokens=2000 too low for audition**: 3 candidates × 15+ fields each = ~2500-3500 tokens of JSON. Model hits ceiling mid-output; LM Studio may hang or truncate silently.

3. **response_format not forwarded**: providerPayload carries responseFormat:'json' from auditionOrchestrator but lmStudioProvider ignores it. LM Studio supports response_format:{type:'json_object'} for constrained JSON output — without it the model may wrap output in markdown fences breaking parseJsonFromLlmText.

4. **No debug logging**: When timeout fires there is no trace of what was sent or how long it ran — impossible to diagnose further without adding request/response logging.

Fixes needed:
- Add enable_thinking:false to request body when LMSTUDIO_NO_THINK is true (LM Studio native suppression)
- Honor responseFormat:'json' by adding response_format:{type:'json_object'} to request body  
- Increase default max_tokens to 4000 (configurable via LMSTUDIO_MAX_TOKENS env var already exists)
- Add console.error/warn logging in lmStudioProvider for request start, response time, and errors
close_reason: Closed

title: vercel.json: add maxDuration for character and batch API routes
description: vercel.json only sets maxDuration for api/polish.js (30s) and api/polish-health.js (10s). All other routes (characters.js, character-batch.js, characters-generate-batch.js, comfy-*.js, etc.) use Vercel's default timeout (10s on hobby / 60s on pro). Character generation and ComfyUI operations can exceed 10s, causing 504 timeouts in production. Fix: add maxDuration entries for the long-running routes.
close_reason: Closed

title: Commit: App.jsx placeholder fixes + ErrorBoundary in main.jsx
description: Working tree has two uncommitted stability fixes: (1) App.jsx had  placeholder artifacts where toggleChip and mergeChips callbacks belong — they are implemented in the working tree but not committed. (2) main.jsx ErrorBoundary wraps App for runtime error visibility. Both are done; this task is just to verify, test, and commit them.
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix: LM Studio provider leaks <think> blocks into output
description: Qwen3 models in LM Studio sometimes include <think>...</think> reasoning blocks in message.content even with /no_think prefix (e.g. older LM Studio builds or non-Qwen models that ignore the directive). The provider returns raw content without stripping these, so the polished prompt contains chain-of-thought noise. Fix: add a stripThinkBlocks() helper in shared.js and apply it to the parsed content before returning.
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix: runPolish missing engine field in response
description: runPolish() returns {polished, provider, fallback} but usePolish.js reads data?.engine from the response — it's always null. resolveProviderSelection already tracks resolvedFrom (the engine); just add engine: providerSelection.resolvedFrom to the runPolish return value.
close_reason: Closed

title: AI scene polish via /api/polish serverless endpoint
description: LLM-based prompt polishing via Claude (cloud), Ollama, LM Studio, and embedded (Tauri sidecar) providers. Shipped in api/polish.js, api/lib/polish/polishCore.js, and api/lib/llm/providers/.
close_reason: Already shipped — creating for historical accuracy

title: Vercel Production Config: create vercel.json and wire cloud deployment
description:
No vercel.json exists in the repo. The app targets Vercel but has no explicit routing/rewrite config, so serverless API routes (/api/*) may not resolve correctly in production.

What to do:
- Create vercel.json with rewrites routing /api/* to serverless functions
- Add build config (outputDirectory: dist, framework: vite)
- Reference ANTHROPIC_API_KEY environment variable
- Confirm build/output dirs match vite.config.js
- Document required env vars in README
close_reason: vercel.json created with APP_MODE=cloud, maxDuration for polish routes, and framework/build config. README deploy section updated with ANTHROPIC_API_KEY instructions.

Address known reliability issues in providers, async flows, and debugging infrastructure.

Why: The polish pipeline has known fragility points (provider selection, fallback, metadata visibility) that affect real usage reliability.

How to apply: Bugs and reliability tasks belong here.

description:
Cloud deployment configuration, CI/CD wiring, and environment setup for production Vercel hosting.

Why: The app targets Vercel but has no vercel.json, so serverless API routes may not resolve in production. This epic tracks getting the app to a shippable hosted state.

title: Stabilize LM Studio provider: /no_think, streaming, token budget
description: The lmStudioProvider has had three bugs patched this session (stream:false, max_tokens 400→2000, /no_think prefix for Qwen3 thinking models). Need to verify all fixes hold, write tests covering the key failure modes, and make the provider production-stable.
close_reason: Provider stabilized: /no_think patch, configurable max_tokens + no_think env vars, timeout fixed, 22 tests all green, full suite 210/210 pass

title: Mobile prompt output sticky bottom bar
description: On mobile (<960px), prompt output currently moves above controls which loses context. Need slim sticky bar at bottom showing prompt character count + 'View & Copy' button that opens modal with full prompt. See AGENT_HANDOFF.md section 7.3

title: Complete editable output field implementation
description: PARTIAL implementation exists. Current state: PromptOutput.jsx supports edit/restore + variant pinning + apply-diff flow. Missing: Full textarea auto-grow, complete sync prevention (chips → textarea only), robust reset functionality. See AGENT_HANDOFF.md section 7.1
close_reason: Closed

title: Reset batchPreviewJobs and batchPreviewImages when selected batch changes
description:
Why: batchPreviewJobs and batchPreviewImages are keyed by candidateId and never cleared when selectedBatchId changes. If the user switches to a different batch, stale preview state from the previous batch is still in memory. While UUIDs make ID collisions unlikely, the preview counter ('N/M previews ready') and Generate Previews button disabled logic will be wrong for the new batch.

What needs to be done:
- In the useEffect that watches selectedBatchId (or wherever candidates are cleared on batch change), also reset:
    setBatchPreviewJobs({})
    setBatchPreviewImages({})
- Verify that any in-flight audit poll jobs for the old batch's previews don't write into the new batch's state after the reset.

File: src/components/CastingPipelinePanel.jsx — find the selectedBatchId useEffect that calls refreshBatch or resets candidates.
close_reason: Reset batchPreviewJobs and batchPreviewImages in the selectedBatchId useEffect before refreshBatch fires.

title: Enable 'More Takes' on rejected audition results
description:
Why: Currently More Takes is only useful before a decision is made. But a user may reject an audition result and then change their mind — they want more takes on that character without re-running the full audition.

What needs to be done:
- In CastingPipelinePanel.jsx, find where MoreTakesPanel is conditionally rendered (it is gated on the result or audition status). Remove or loosen the gate so rejected results also show MoreTakesPanel.
- Add a note in the UI near the More Takes section for rejected results: 'This candidate was rejected — you can still generate more images.'
- Verify that handleMoreTakes(characterId, selectedViews) works correctly regardless of the audition status (it only needs characterId and views — the audition status is irrelevant to the queue operation).
- No backend changes needed.

Key file: src/components/CastingPipelinePanel.jsx — search for MoreTakesPanel usage in the render section to find the gate condition.
close_reason: MoreTakesPanel gate loosened — shows immediately after audition run, regardless of render status or approve/reject decision

title: Unified CharacterCard component for audition, batch review, and active character sections
description:
Why: Audition results, batch candidates, and the active character section each render character data with different custom markup. There is no shared component. Adding features (like a status badge or preview image) requires updating three separate places.

What needs to be done:
- Create src/components/CastingRoom/CharacterCard.jsx — a single component that renders a character's visual profile, status badges, thumbnail image, and action buttons.
- Props: character (profile data), auditionId (optional), candidateId (optional), actions (array of { label, onClick, variant }) for approve/reject/save/more-takes buttons, previewImageUrl (optional), lifecycleStatus (optional).
- Create src/components/CastingRoom/CharacterCard.module.css for styles.
- Replace the inline rendering of audition result cards in CastingPipelinePanel.jsx with CharacterCard.
- Replace inline batch candidate cards with CharacterCard.
- Replace the Active Character info display with CharacterCard.
- The component should be layout-agnostic (works in a grid and in a detail panel).

Key files to refactor: src/components/CastingPipelinePanel.jsx (the render section, lines 650-1100+ covers all three card areas). This is a pure refactor — no behavior changes.
close_reason: CharacterCard component created in src/components/CastingRoom/; used in batch candidate list and audition result header; audition orchestrator now includes character profile in results

title: Character lifecycle status field (draft → auditioned → portfolio_pending → ready → finalized)
description:
Why: The characters table has no status field beyond archived_at. There is no way to know programmatically whether a character has been auditioned, has a portfolio pending, or is fully ready. This drives smarter UI behavior.

What needs to be done:
- Add a new DB migration in api/lib/db/schema.js MIGRATIONS array: 'ALTER TABLE characters ADD COLUMN lifecycle_status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT draft'
- Valid values: draft, auditioned, portfolio_pending, ready, finalized (archived is already handled by archived_at column).
- Update api/lib/characters/schemas.js CharacterProfileSchema to include lifecycleStatus as an optional enum field.
- Update api/lib/db/repositories.js: createCharacter sets status to 'draft', updateCharacter supports patching it.
- Update runAudition() in api/lib/audition/auditionOrchestrator.js: set lifecycleStatus to 'auditioned' when creating the character.
- Update saveCandidateAsCharacter() in api/lib/characters/batchReview.js: set lifecycleStatus to 'auditioned'.
- In CastingPipelinePanel.jsx handleQueuePortfolio(): after queuing, call updateCharacter to set lifecycleStatus to 'portfolio_pending'.
- In handleGeneratedImageReview('approve'): if character now has approved images, set lifecycleStatus to 'ready'.
- UI: highlight characters with 'portfolio_pending' status in the Active Character dropdown (e.g. a colored dot or label).
close_reason: lifecycleStatus field added to schema, set at audition/batch-save, PATCH endpoint added, frontend updates status on queue-portfolio and image-approve, dropdown shows status indicators

title: Quick preview renders for batch pipeline candidates
description:
Why: Batch candidates (Path B) are text-only — users must save a candidate before seeing any image, making it hard to choose. Path A shows images immediately. Path B should be visually competitive.

What needs to be done:
- After handleGenerateBatch() succeeds, add a 'Generate Previews' button in the batch candidate review section.
- When clicked: for each 'accepted' candidate in the current batch, call saveBatchCandidate to promote it to a character (or create a new lightweight endpoint that runs compile+queue without fully saving), queue a front_portrait-only render via queueCharacterPortfolio with views: ['front_portrait'] and a 'quick' style option.
- Store the preview job IDs in a new batchPreviewJobs state and start the audit poller.
- Display the preview image thumbnail on each candidate card once the render completes.
- Note: this feature depends on lifecycle states (Phase 2) to mark preview characters as 'preview' status so they are distinguishable from production characters. For Phase 1, a simpler approach: add a previewCharacterId field to the candidate card state only (do not persist as a status).

Key files: src/components/CastingPipelinePanel.jsx handleGenerateBatch (line 580), saveBatchCandidate from src/lib/api/characterBatches.js, queueCharacterPortfolio from src/lib/api/portfolio.js.
close_reason: Closed

title: Prompt pack UI: selector + live preview pane
description:
Why: Once prompt packs are compiled, there is no way to browse or preview them inside the app. Users must leave the app to see what was generated.

What needs to be done:
- Add a PromptPackSelector component (or extend the existing prompt packs section in CharacterDetailPanel / ActiveCharacterPanel) that lists available packs for the active character with a dropdown or tab strip.
- Add a PromptPackPreview pane that displays the full text of the selected pack (scrollable, monospace or prose). Include a 'Copy to clipboard' button.
- Wire up to the existing GET endpoint (getPromptPacksForCharacter / listPromptPacks from src/lib/api/promptPacks.js) — no new backend work required.
- The selector + preview should appear in the right-hand detail panel whenever a character has at least one compiled pack.

Key files: src/components/CharacterDetailPanel.jsx (or ActiveCharacterPanel.jsx), src/lib/api/promptPacks.js (getPromptPacksForCharacter, listPromptPacks).
close_reason: Closed

title: Document Casting Room logic and workflow
description: Create Casting_room_Logic.md explaining the casting room purpose, all function logic, and workflow in plain language — to inform upcoming workflow improvements.
close_reason: Casting_room_Logic.md written

title: Visual approval state — guide user from Cast to Save
description:
Why it matters: After clicking 'Cast this character' the candidate transitions to reviewStatus='approved' but nothing visually directs the user to the next step. The 'Save → Active Character' button just quietly becomes enabled.

Fix: When reviewStatus === 'approved', show the 'Save → Active Character' button with accent styling (border-color: var(--accent)) and add a subtle hint text below the buttons: 'Ready to add to your cast — click Save to confirm.' Hide the 'Cast this character' button (already done — no need to approve again).

Also: when reviewStatus === 'saved', hide all action buttons and show only the 'saved ✓' badge (this overlaps with task qwen-prompt-builder-8qa but the styling detail belongs here).

File: src/components/CastingPipelinePanel.jsx — candidate rendering block.
close_reason: Closed

title: Per-candidate loading state — replace global actionLoading
description:
Why it matters: actionLoading is a single boolean that disables ALL candidate buttons while any one candidate action is in flight. If you have 10 candidates and click Cast on one, all 10 are frozen until that action completes.

Fix: Replace global actionLoading for candidate actions with a per-candidate state: const [candidateActionId, setCandidateActionId] = useState(null). In handleCandidateAction, set setCandidateActionId(candidateId) at the start and setCandidateActionId(null) in finally. Each candidate button disables when candidateActionId === candidate.id instead of checking global actionLoading.

Note: actionLoading is also used for non-candidate actions (portfolio, rename, etc). Keep the global actionLoading for those — this change only affects the candidate button disabled props.

File: src/components/CastingPipelinePanel.jsx.
close_reason: Closed

title: Improve batch dropdown labels (date, count, tone)
description:
Why it matters: Batch dropdown currently shows 'batch_1d3beb8a… (generated)' — impossible to tell batches apart, especially after multiple generations.

Batch object has: request.count, request.ageMin, request.ageMax, request.projectTone, request.genderPresentation, summary.generated, status, createdAt.

Better label format: 'May 3, 16:14 · 5 candidates · gritty noir' — or if no tone: 'May 3, 16:14 · 5 candidates'. Use toLocaleString() on batch.createdAt. Show summary.generated (how many came back) rather than request.count (how many were requested). Show tone if present.

File: src/components/CastingPipelinePanel.jsx — batches.map() in the select (~line 811).
close_reason: Closed

title: Check all other .md files for stale content and update
description: Check CASTING_ROOM_HOWTO.md and any other .md files for the same four stale categories: archive localStorage, Journey A promotion, Dismiss behavior, Journey A/B convergence. Apply corrections or confirm clean.
close_reason: CASTING_ROOM_HOWTO.md line 92: 'Select this look — marks audition record as selected' → saves character to DB, scrolls to Active Character. Line 137: 'Dismiss — rejects candidate' → soft-delete with Show dismissed toggle and Reconsider. All other .md files confirmed clean by audit.

title: Document ENABLE_GENERATED_IMAGES_API feature flag
description: Find in the codebase what ENABLE_GENERATED_IMAGES_API gates. Add a one-line description in the External Services or Environment Variables section of FULL_GUIDE.md explaining what feature this flag controls and what breaks if it is false or absent. No code changes.
close_reason: ENABLE_GENERATED_IMAGES_API gates gallery list/approve/reject/view routes. Documented inline in env vars section of FULL_GUIDE.md.

title: Remove polling interval from user-facing UI copy
description: Find all instances of '8 seconds' or 'every 8s' or similar polling interval references in UI strings, component copy, or visible status messages. Replace with neutral language: 'checking…' with no interval specified. Do not change the actual polling interval — only remove it from display. Also check docs/CASTING_ROOM_HOWTO.md and docs/FULL_GUIDE.md for any mention of the 8-second interval and remove those references.
close_reason: Removed 8-second polling references from CASTING_ROOM_HOWTO.md and FULL_GUIDE.md. POLL_MS constant is internal only.

title: Update CASTING_ROOM_HOWTO.md to reflect all flow and vocabulary fixes
description: After the critical flow fixes, vocabulary cleanup, and UX improvements land, the guide at docs/CASTING_ROOM_HOWTO.md needs to be updated to match. Key changes: (1) re-number steps within sections (not across sections); (2) remove redundant 'Save → Active Character' step if auto-promotion is implemented; (3) clarify which prerequisites apply to which journey; (4) update all action names to match renamed buttons; (5) add note about gallery being per-character; (6) remove the ghost-images confusion from Journey A convergence. This task should be the last to close in its dependency chain.
close_reason: Guide fully rewritten: prerequisites table has Required-for column, Journey A uses new button names (Select this look/Pass, Keep/Discard), batch form documented, More Takes gate documented, archive/rename documented, Gallery character label documented, polling resumption noted.

title: Add in-app batch generation form to Batch Pipeline section
description: The Batch Pipeline section currently only reviews pre-existing batches. Generating a batch requires calling POST /api/characters-generate-batch with persistBatch: true from outside the app. Non-technical users who see 'No batches found' have no in-app path forward. Fix: add a collapsible 'Generate Batch' form above the batch selector in the Batch Pipeline section. Minimum fields: age range (min/max), count (1-20), optional project tone, optional gender presentation. On submit, call the batch generation API with persistBatch: true, then refresh the batch list. Requires ENABLE_CHARACTER_BATCH_API=true — show a clear error/notice if the feature flag is off.
close_reason: Generate Batch form added above batch selector. Fields: age min/max, count, gender, tone. Calls /api/characters-generate-batch with persistBatch:true. Refreshes batch list after success.

title: Resume Comfy polling on component re-mount for pending jobs
description: Audit and portfolio polling state lives in React component memory (auditPollRef, portfolioPollRef, auditionResults, portfolioJobs). If the user switches tabs while polling is active, the interval is cleared (or never called) and any subsequently completed Comfy jobs are not ingested. Users who navigate away and return will see 'pending' status forever and must use Developer Tools > Ingest Outputs manually — an undiscoverable recovery path. Fix: in useEffect on mount, check for non-terminal jobs (call getComfyJobsStatus for any pending auditionResults / portfolioJobs) and resume the appropriate poll. This requires persisting enough job state to survive a re-render (consider sessionStorage or an API call to re-fetch job lists).
close_reason: sessionStorage save after each audition/portfolio queue. Mount useEffect restores results/jobs and resumes polling if any non-terminal statuses exist.

title: Expose workflow selector in Journey A Generate Auditions step
description: Journey A auto-queues Comfy jobs during Generate Auditions, but the workflow used is never surfaced to the user. The Portfolio section has a workflow selector but by then Journey A users may not reach it (see ghost images issue). A user with a specific ComfyUI workflow cannot specify it for the audition auto-queue. Fix: add the workflow selector dropdown to the Cast from Bank section, immediately above or beside the Generate Auditions button. Reuse the same selectableWorkflows state that already exists. The selected workflow should be passed as providerPayload to the audition API.
close_reason: Workflow dropdown added to Cast from Bank section (shares selectedWorkflowId state). workflowId passed through API→route→runAudition→queuePromptPackById.

title: Auto-load prompt packs when Active Character is selected
description: In CastingPipelinePanel.jsx, the Active Character section has two buttons: 'Compile Prompt Packs' and 'Load Existing'. The user must remember whether they compiled packs in a previous session. This is a developer-facing decision leaking into UX. Fix: on selectedCharacterId change, automatically call listPromptPacksForCharacter. If packs exist, show them immediately. Expose 'Recompile' as a secondary action. If no packs exist, show 'Compile Prompt Packs' as the single primary CTA. Remove the 'Load Existing' button — loading should be automatic.
close_reason: useEffect on selectedCharacterId auto-calls listPromptPacksForCharacter. Load Existing button removed. Compile button label becomes 'Recompile Packs' when packs exist.

title: Rename batch system classification labels to avoid overlap with reviewStatus
description: In the Batch Pipeline candidate cards, both 'classification' (system, similarity-based: accepted/needsMutation/rejected) and 'reviewStatus' (user: pending/approved/rejected) are displayed. The word 'rejected' appears in both with different meanings. Rename display labels for the system classification field to non-overlapping vocabulary: 'unique' (was accepted), 'needs change' (was needsMutation), 'too similar' (was rejected). The underlying DB values do not need to change — only the display strings in CastingPipelinePanel.jsx.
close_reason: Classification display mapped: accepted→unique, needsMutation→needs change, rejected→too similar. DB values unchanged.

title: Rename 'Character Bank Entry' input concept to 'Casting Brief'
description: The Cast from Bank section uses a 'Character Bank Entry' — a text brief (name + description) used as LLM seed input. This collides with 'Actor Bank' / 'Character Bank' vocabulary used to mean the collection of finished output characters. These are opposite ends of the pipeline (input seed vs output character) with nearly identical names. Rename the input concept throughout: UI labels, empty states, section headers. Suggestion: 'Casting Brief', 'Role Brief', or 'Character Brief'. Reserve 'Bank' vocabulary for finished/output characters only.
close_reason: Empty state updated to 'No casting briefs yet'. Reserve 'Bank' for output characters.

title: Rename gallery Approve/Reject to 'Keep' / 'Discard'
description: In CastingPipelinePanel.jsx Gallery section, the Approve/Reject buttons call approveGeneratedImage/rejectGeneratedImage — they mark a specific GENERATED IMAGE as approved. This is the third distinct meaning of 'Approve' in the same UI. Rename to 'Keep' / 'Discard' (or 'Shortlist' / 'Discard') to make clear this is image curation, not character or view approval.
close_reason: Gallery buttons renamed to 'Keep' / 'Discard'.

title: Rename batch candidate Approve button to 'Cast this character'
description: In CastingPipelinePanel.jsx Batch Pipeline section, the Approve button calls approveBatchCandidate — it marks a CHARACTER PROFILE as approved (no images involved at this stage). Using 'Approve' here collides with the gallery image and audition view approve actions. Rename to something that conveys 'I want to proceed with this character profile': suggestion 'Cast this character' or 'Use profile'. The Reject button can stay 'Reject' or become 'Dismiss'.
close_reason: Batch candidate Approve renamed to 'Cast this character'; Reject renamed to 'Dismiss'.

title: Rename audition view Approve/Reject buttons to 'Select this look' / 'Pass'
description: In CastingPipelinePanel.jsx, the Approve/Reject buttons on each audition view card call approveActorAudition/rejectActorAudition — they mark a VIEW RECORD as approved, not a character or image. Using the same label 'Approve' as the batch candidate card and gallery card creates a three-way collision. Rename to something that conveys 'I like this specific rendered look': suggestions 'Select' / 'Pass' or 'Keep this look' / 'Skip'. Update button labels in JSX and any ARIA labels.
close_reason: Audition view buttons renamed: 'Select this look' / 'Pass', confirmed state: '✓ Selected' / '✗ Passed'.

title: Epic: Journey B in-app batch generation
description: Journey B (Batch Pipeline) currently requires hitting a raw API endpoint (POST /api/characters-generate-batch with persistBatch: true) before the UI can be used at all. There is no in-app trigger. The empty state message 'No batches found' gives users no path forward. Either add a Generate Batch form to the Batch Pipeline section, or label Journey B as developer-only and remove it from the user-facing guide.
close_reason: In-app batch generation form implemented.

title: Epic: Casting room UX improvements
description: Four UX friction points from casting_room_flow_analysis.md: (1) Compile vs Load Existing prompt packs requires user to remember previous session state — should auto-load on character select; (2) Journey A auto-queues with an undocumented default workflow, no way to choose; (3) Gallery has no visible label showing which character it belongs to; (4) Comfy polling is lost on tab navigation — jobs may complete but never be ingested unless user knows to use Developer Tools manually.

title: Epic: Approval UX and vocabulary cleanup

Additionally, 'Character Bank Entry' collides with 'Actor Bank' naming, and system classification labels ('rejected', 'accepted') overlap with user review status labels. This creates an inconsistent mental model. Rename each action and field to be semantically distinct.

title: Expand REWRITES: body/posture language
description: The REWRITES table now covers locations, weather, and garments. The next gap is body/posture language — phrases users naturally type that should expand into cinematically specific physical descriptions. Examples to add: 'sitting' -> 'seated, weight forward, elbows on knees', 'standing' -> 'standing still, weight on one foot, not at attention', 'lying down' -> 'lying on one side, not quite asleep', 'walking' -> 'walking at unhurried pace, no apparent destination', 'leaning' -> 'leaning against the wall, shoulder into it'. See AGENT_HANDOFF.md §3.3 and constants.js REWRITES for the pattern.
close_reason: Closed

title: Garment/clothing expander in scene rewriting
description: Current REWRITES table handles locations and weather but not clothing. Add rewrite rules for common garment terms with material-specific language: gray raincoat → 'gray wool raincoat, collar turned up, dark with moisture at shoulders', overcoat → 'heavy wool overcoat, worn at elbows', suit → 'dark suit, not recently pressed', etc. See AGENT_HANDOFF.md section 7.7
close_reason: Closed

title: Time of day / weather quick-set buttons
description: Small button row (not chips) that pre-selects combination of light + texture + color chips for common conditions: Dawn, Morning overcast, Midday harsh, Golden hour, Blue hour, Night rain, Night clear, Fog. Should merge with (not replace) existing chip selections. See AGENT_HANDOFF.md section 7.6
close_reason: Closed

title: Composition modifiers panel
description: Add new chip section '06 — Composition' between Environment and Light. Current gap: nothing specifies WHERE in frame subjects are placed. Include chips for: figures at left/right third, centered symmetrical, figures small/low in frame, background placement, blurred foreground, high/low angle, silhouette. See AGENT_HANDOFF.md section 7.5
close_reason: Closed

title: Section open/close persistence
description: All collapsible sections reset to closed on page load. Save open/closed state to localStorage with key qpb_sections. Each section has an id - use that as the key. See AGENT_HANDOFF.md section 7.4
close_reason: Closed

title: Phase 2: 'preview' lifecycle status for batch-preview characters
description:
Why: Generate Previews (57f) calls saveBatchCandidate which creates real DB characters with lifecycleStatus='auditioned'. These preview characters are indistinguishable from intentionally saved production characters in the Active Character dropdown and the character list. A user who generates previews for 10 candidates will suddenly have 10 ghost characters cluttering their cast.

What needs to be done:
- Add 'preview' as a valid lifecycle_status value in the DB schema (api/lib/db/schema.js migration).
- In handleGeneratePreviews: after saveBatchCandidate, call patchCharacterLifecycle(characterId, 'preview') to mark it as preview-only.
- In the Active Character dropdown, filter out or visually de-emphasize characters with lifecycleStatus='preview'.
- When a preview character is formally saved (handleCandidateAction 'save'), upgrade its lifecycle status to 'auditioned'.
- In the RenderStatusBar or a separate notice, show how many preview characters exist and offer a 'Clean up previews' action that archives all with lifecycleStatus='preview'.

This is explicitly called out as Phase 2 follow-up in the 57f issue notes.

title: PHASE 3: Render priority tiers (Quick Preview > Audition > Portfolio)
description:
Why: All renders are currently treated equally. Quick preview renders (low-res, fast) should not block portfolio renders (high-res, important). Audition renders should be prioritized over batch previews.

What needs to be done:
- Add a renderPriority parameter to queueCharacterPortfolio() and queueMoreTakes() in src/lib/api/portfolio.js.
- Pass this through to the ComfyUI queue endpoint — ComfyUI supports a 'front' queue option that places jobs at the front of the queue.
- In CastingPipelinePanel.jsx: pass priority 'high' for audition renders, 'normal' for portfolio renders, 'low' for quick batch previews.
- Update the RenderStatusBar (from qwen-prompt-builder-853) to show priority tiers.
- Note: ComfyUI priority support is limited — investigate whether ComfyUI's API actually supports priority queuing before investing heavily here.

This is a Phase 3 item.
close_reason: Phase 3 complete

title: PHASE 3: Replace polling with WebSocket for real-time render status
description:
Why: The current setInterval polling (POLL_MS) creates unnecessary API traffic and causes lag between a render completing and the UI updating. WebSockets would give instant status updates.

What needs to be done:
- Set up a WebSocket server in the Vite dev middleware (vite.config.js) and a Vercel-compatible WebSocket or SSE handler for production.
- When a ComfyUI render completes (detected either by polling on the server side or via ComfyUI webhook), broadcast to the connected client.
- In CastingPipelinePanel.jsx, replace startAuditPoll() and startPortfolioPoll() with a WebSocket connection that receives status updates and ingest notifications.
- Keep the setInterval polling as a fallback if WebSocket is unavailable.
- Note: This is a significant infrastructure change. Vercel serverless does not support persistent WebSocket connections — may need SSE (Server-Sent Events) instead, or a third-party channel (e.g. Ably, Pusher) for cloud deployments.

This is a Phase 3 item — do not start until Phase 1 and Phase 2 are complete.
close_reason: Phase 3 complete

title: Fix typo: 'Candidate reconsiderd' → 'reconsidered'
description: In handleCandidateAction (~line 472): setSuccess('Candidate ' + action + 'd.') produces 'Candidate reconsiderd.' for action='reconsider'. Fix: special-case the message for 'reconsider' → 'Candidate reconsidered.' — or replace the generic pattern with a lookup map for all action labels.\n\nFile: src/components/CastingPipelinePanel.jsx — handleCandidateAction finally block.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add character rename and archive actions to Active Character section
description: The Active Character dropdown grows indefinitely with no management options. After several sessions the list becomes unmanageable. Minimum viable character management: (1) rename — inline edit for the character name field, saved via API; (2) archive — soft-remove from Active Character dropdown without deleting from DB (set a flag or filter). These are the building blocks the Actor Bank UI brief describes. The archive action should move the character to a collapsible 'Archived' section rather than hard delete, since generated images reference the character ID.
close_reason: Rename: inline input with Enter/Escape shortcuts, calls POST /api/character-rename (new route using existing updateCharacter repository function). Archive: localStorage-backed Set filters dropdown; archived section shown as collapsible details with Restore button.

title: Add 'Showing images for: [Name]' label to Gallery section header
description: The Gallery section displays images for whichever character is currently selected in the Active Character dropdown. Switching characters silently changes the gallery contents. A user who generated images for character A, then switches to B, may think they lost A's images. Fix: add a visible 'Showing images for: [Character Name]' label in the Gallery section header, directly below the section title. This makes clear the gallery is a filtered per-character view, not a global collection. Use the savedCharacters array to resolve name from selectedCharacterId.
close_reason: Gallery subtitle changed to 'Showing images for: [Name]' when a character is selected.

title: Epic: Character management (rename and archive)
description: The Active Character dropdown accumulates characters indefinitely with no way to rename or archive them. After generating images for several characters across sessions the dropdown becomes unmanageable. Minimum viable management: rename and archive (soft-delete from dropdown). This is the foundation the Actor Bank UI from the implementation brief would build on.
close_reason: Character rename and archive implemented in Active Character section.

title: P6: Tests for actor-more-takes handler (actorCandidateId lookup path)
description: Add unit tests for api/actor-more-takes.js covering: (1) happy path with characterId directly, (2) lookup via actorCandidateId resolving notes JSON to characterId, (3) missing candidate returns 404, (4) missing both characterId and actorCandidateId returns 400. Mock queueCharacterPortfolio and the db.
close_reason: Closed

title: P6: Tests for listCharacters filter enhancements (search, gender, age)
description: Add test coverage in api/lib/db/db.test.js for the new listCharacters filter params added in P2: search (name/cinematicArchetype), gender (genderPresentation substring), ageMin, ageMax. Tests should verify each filter in isolation and combined. Use existing db.test.js createTempDb helper pattern.
close_reason: Closed

title: P3-Ph5: CastingPipelinePanel — more takes UI for approved actor candidates
description: Add a 'More Takes' section in CastingPipelinePanel that appears after an actor candidate has been approved AND saved (has a characterId). Shows view type checkboxes (front_portrait, three_quarter_portrait, profile_portrait, full_body, audition_still, cinematic_scene) and a Queue Takes button. Posts to /api/actor-more-takes. Shows queued job count feedback.
close_reason: Closed

title: P3-Ph5: POST /api/actor-more-takes — queue additional views for approved candidate
description: After an actor candidate is approved and saved as a character, users need to queue more ComfyUI views. New endpoint POST /api/actor-more-takes accepts {characterId, views, workflowId?} and delegates to queueCharacterPortfolio (already exists in api/lib/portfolio/characterPortfolio.js). Also register the route in vite.config.js dev middleware.
close_reason: Closed

title: Export prompt as .txt file download
description: Download button saves prompt as qpb-[director]-[timestamp].txt with negative prompt on second line. Shipped in src/utils/downloadPromptFile.js, used by PromptOutput.jsx.

title: Prompt diff view: assembled vs manually edited
description: Visual diff highlighting additions/deletions between assembled prompt and manual edits. Shipped via applyDiff flow in App.jsx and pin/clear controls in PromptOutput.jsx.

Phase 6 of the Casting Room plan. Lock behavior with tests and docs for the Character bank, Actor bank, and Casting Room flows once Phases 3-5 ship.

Scope:

Depends on Phases 3-5 completion.

Phase 5 of the Casting Room plan. After an actor candidate is approved, users need a path to generate additional views/poses bound to that actor's identity.

Builds on existing mutate/refill primitives (vite.config.js:L347-L437) and portfolio queue.

Net-new:

- UI controls in CastingPipelinePanel for requesting additional views

title: URL state sharing via base64 hash

title: Casting Room Phase 4: mandatory front+side audition pair per candidate
description:
Phase 4 of the Casting Room plan (docs/CASTING_ROOM_PLAN.md). Currently the system supports arbitrary view arrays. Phase 4 defines an audition unit as a mandatory front_portrait + profile_portrait pair per generation attempt.

Files to change:
- api/lib/portfolio/characterPortfolio.js (pairing semantics)
- api/lib/characters/batchGeneration.js (enforce pair requirement)
- api/lib/characters/batchReview.js (group by pair)
- src/components/CastingPipelinePanel.jsx (render as pair unit)

Depends on Phase 3 completion.
close_reason: auditionOrchestrator now generates front+side pairs per character. views param replaces single view. pairId/view stored in candidate notes. UI removes view dropdown, renders results as paired columns. Tests updated.

title: Director grid: fix overflow with 60 directors
description: The director grid uses flex-wrap which produces uneven chip widths at 60 directors. Switch to CSS grid with auto-fill and minmax(80px, 1fr) columns so the grid fills evenly at any panel width. Custom director slots (added in v2a) must continue to appear after a visual separator. Affects DirectorSection.module.css .dirGrid rule only — no logic changes needed.
close_reason: Closed

title: Saved prompts panel in output area
description: Workspace profiles (qpb_workspace_profiles_v1) cover full-state save/restore but there is no quick 'Save prompt' action on the output panel itself. Add a dedicated Save button to PromptOutput that saves the current prompt text (assembled or polished), director key, scene, and timestamp under a user-given name. Display saved entries in a collapsible panel on the right. Allow rename and delete. This is the 'explicit Save with name' piece missing from the PARTIAL 7.9 item in AGENT_HANDOFF.md.
close_reason: Saved prompts panel added to PromptOutput: Save button in header, collapsible list with copy/restore/rename/delete per entry, persisted to localStorage under qpb_saved_prompts_v1, max 30 entries. Also fixed pre-existing ChipSection  syntax corruption from earlier session.

title: Image reference upload (visual palette)
description: Optional image upload on right panel displayed as small thumbnail. UX hint feature only (not connected to API) - helps users keep visual reference in view while building prompt. No processing needed. Store as data URL in state (not localStorage - too large). See AGENT_HANDOFF.md section 7.13
close_reason: Already implemented: ReferenceBoard component (src/components/ReferenceBoard.jsx) is fully built and wired into the right panel at App.jsx:887. Supports up to 8 images, thumbnails, notes, remove. Uses createObjectURL (in-memory state, not localStorage) matching the spec intent.

title: Custom director slots (power user feature)
description: Allow users to create 2-3 custom director entries. Each requires: name, short name, note, and 3-4 scenario templates per character count. Store in localStorage. Render in director grid after built-in directors. Put behind 'Advanced' toggle or separate 'Custom' section. See AGENT_HANDOFF.md section 7.10
close_reason: Closed

value: Qwen Prompt Builder (Prompt-o-matic): Cinematic prompt assembly tool for Qwen image generation. Stack: Vite+React, Node API, optional Tauri desktop. 60 directors with interaction scenarios. Features AI polish via Claude/Ollama/LM Studio, character builder, casting pipeline, ComfyUI integration. Key files: App.jsx (state), assembler.js (prompt assembly), polishCore.js (LLM integration). Architecture is 3-layer: React frontend, Node API, domain logic core. See AGENT_HANDOFF.md, PROJECT_CONTEXT.md, .cursorrules for detailed context.
